constants.const: Audio classes interface with audio hardware and drivers for audio playback and recording purposes.
Command classes perform specific procedures, like copying or moving a file, managing volumes or executing a program.
Data classes parse, query and manipulate data.
FileSystem classes are based on file management and interaction with file based data.
Graphics classes provide graphics management and drawing services.
GUI classes are used in the development of graphical user interfaces.
I/O classes manage hardware and software based input and output.
Miscellaneous classes do not fit into any of the other available categories.
Classes that represent more than one media type, e.g. video files.
Network classes interface with system drivers to simplify network communications.
System classes are designed to provide low-level services related to system management.
Tools provide interactive services for the user.
Copy from the clipboard.
Cut from the clipboard.
Paste from the clipboard.
Audio file data, recognised by the Sound class
Document content (between XML tags) - sent by document objects only
Device activity
File location (the data will reflect the complete file path)
Image file data, recognised by the Image class
Device input that has been transformed into user input
Raw unprocessed data
Receipt for item data, in response to an earlier request
Database record
Make a request for item data
Standard ASCII text
Markup based text data. NOTE - For clipboard data, the top-level encapsulating tag must declare the type of XML, e.g. 'html', 'ripple'. For plain XML, use 'xml'
This read-only flag is set if the UI has been disabled through the Disable action.
Allow direct keyboard input and document editing.
Turn off debug output produced during document layout and processing - useful on refresh for example.
System-keys provide standard key support for Ctrl-C, Ctrl-X etc. Set this flag to turn them off.
This flag forces overwrite mode when the user enters information through the keyboard. If the flag is not set, then insert mode is used.
Turn off all security measures - may only be set prior to initialisation.

constants.comment: Event flags for selectively receiving events from the Document object.
constants.const: The user has interacted with a hyperlink. This event can be cancelled by returning ERR::Skip.
The user has interacted with an element that has an on-click definition.
Synonym for ON_CROSSING_IN | ON_CROSSING_OUT
The mouse pointer has crossed into an element.
The mouse pointer has crossed out of an element.
The user has triggered a motion event in an element that supports motion monitoring.
The source file path has changed. Useful for detecting when the user has left the page.
The state value of a widget has changed (e.g. input, checkbox, text). name and value keys will be defined in the event parameters.
This marker indicates that the presented volume is a bookmark and not a device name.
Compact disc style device
Catch-all for fixed media devices that are not directly identifiable.
Floppy disk style device
Hard disk style device
Device is RAM, ROM, WORM, NVRAM, flashdisk or other form of memory. Does not guarantee a fast connection as it could be over a slow USB 1.1 connection for example
Device is a modem.
Device represents a network link
Device is a paper-based printer.
Device is a three dimensional printer.
Device is readable
Device media is removable from the hardware
Device media is removable from the hardware
Device is a two dimensional scanner.
Device is a three dimensional scanner.
Device is virtual/software defined
Tape/Stream style device
All storage is temporary
Removable USB storage device. May be further qualified by HARD_DISK, FLOPPY_DISK etc
Device is writeable
Android specific events that do not already fit existing categories.
Custom event dispatched from an application
Audio system events.
Custom event dispatched from a class that doesn't fit within the rest of the event framework
Video display events.
File system events.
Events generated by the Graphical User Interface.
Hardware device events that are not covered by other types.
Input/Output events.
Network events.
Power Management - can also include app-specific events relating to resource management.
System-wide events
User activity events (such as user login).
Indicates the returned value is a dynamic allocation that needs to be manually freed (by default is a memory pointer, can be an object if combined with FD_OBJECT)
Indicates an array of values. Follow with ARRAYSIZE if used in a function.
FUNCDEF Overrides LOOKUP. Pair with ARRAY to indicate total elements in the array. It is preferred that any null terminator is not counted. If ARRAYSIZE not specified, ARRAY must be null-terminated.
FUNCDEF Overrides WRITE. Indicates a buffer that can be filled with data by the function if paired with RESULT; must be paired with BUFSIZE in second argument.
FUNCDEF Overrides LOOKUP. Pair with BUFFER to indicate the byte size of the buffer memory.
8 bit integer.
All types that directly contribute to the core definition of a C/C++ type
Use the C++ variant of the indicated type, e.g. ARRAY is a std::vector.
64 bit float.
For actions and methods
FUNCDEF
CLASSDEF
32 bit float. Not valid for use in class definitions.
Intended for callbacks, uses the rkFunction structure.
Synonym for PTR | FUNCTION
Synonym for INIT
CLASSDEF
32 bit integer.
64 bit integer.
For actions and methods
For actions and methods
CLASSDEF Pointer to an object that is local to the class
CLASSDEF

FUNCDEF Overrides VIRTUAL. The function will overwrite incoming BUFFER or STRING content. If used in conjunction with the RESULT flag, it indicates that the returned content is volatile (valid for a limited time).
Supplementary, can be used with ints (for IDs) or pointers
Synonym for INT | OBJECT
Synonym for PTR | OBJECT
Pointer (32 or 64 bit).
Synonym for SYSTEM
Synonym for POINTER
The next argument should be INT|BUFSIZE
Use for pointer-based value results only
Synonym for BUFSIZE
Use for pointer-based value results only.
Use for pointer-based value results only.
Use for pointer-based value results only.
Synonym for READ
CLASSDEF
The referenced struct or pointer is a special resource. Changes some behaviours, e.g. a resourced struct will use direct value references rather than being copied in Tiri.
FUNCDEF Overrides READ
Supplementary, if an int type then format is 0xAARRGGBB, if pointer then refers to an RGB structure.
Synonym for READ | INIT
Synonym for READ | WRITE
Supplementary, indicates a float value for multiplicative scaling.
Synonym for STRING
Pointer to a string.
Synonym for STR | RESULT
Supplementary, defines the field as a structure reference. MUST be combined with FD_POINTER. If used in a class, it is possible to name the structure definition in field->Arg
CLASSDEF Use to declare fields that duplicate the functionality of another field.
CLASSDEF System, internal and private fields.
FUNCDEF Overrides INIT
A value that describes a display unit.
Supplementary, integer value is unsigned.
FUNCDEF Overrides FLAGS. Use for 64-bit taglists that require value definitions, e.g. TDOUBLE, TINT etc.
CLASSDEF Ensures that the field has no physical representation in the structure.
Synonym for WRITE
16 bit integer.
CLASSDEF

constants.comment: These are document style flags, as used in the DocStyle structure
constants.const: Align to the centre.
Align to the right.
Do not wrap the text.
All white-space is taken into account.
Synonym for UNDERLINE
Draw a line under the text.

constants.comment: JET constants are documented in GetInputEvent()
constants.const: The X, Y values are defined as absolute coordinates, relative to the top-left of the display.
Left mouse button; XBox A button, PS square button. Value is pressure sensitive, ranging between 0 - 1.0 (0 is released, 1.0 is fully depressed).
Non-specific button assignment.
Right mouse button; XBox X button, PS cross button.
Middle mouse button; XBox Y button, PS triangle.
Alt. mouse button 1; XBox B button, PS circle.
Alt. mouse button 2.
Non-specific button assignment.
Non-specific button assignment.
Non-specific button assignment.
Non-specific button assignment.
This message is sent by the input system when the mouse pointer enters an area for the first time. The message value refers to the object ID of the container being monitored for movement.
This message is sent by the input system when the mouse pointer leaves an area. The message value refers to the object ID of the container being monitored for movement.
Controller tilted on the X/Y axis. Value indicates angle, -ve = left, +ve = right
Controller is rising or falling. Value expressed as 'speed',
Recently supplied input occurred at the edge of the display.
Pen tilt angle. 0 is pointing down directly with nib at bottom, 0.5 is 90 degrees, 1.0 is inversed (eraser at bottom)
Amount of pressure applied, ranges from 0 (none) to 1.0 (normal) and possibly higher if user presses hard enough
Mouse wheel rotation - the value generally reflects the number of 'clicks' rotated on the wheel.
Some mouse wheels can be tilted to the left or right. Ranges from -1.0 to +1.0

constants.comment: JTYPE flags are used to categorise input types.
constants.const: Analog movement (ranging from -1.0 to 1.0)
Cursor has been anchored with LockCursor()
Input is a physical button or switch
Crossing events manage the entering and leaving of an area.
Set by the input system if the Type is a button and the button has been clicked in quick succession so as to be classed as a double-click.
D-Pad or digital joystick source (restricted to +/- 1)
Set if sufficient movement occurred between the original click and its point of release (usually requires a 3 or more pixel difference).
This special flag is set by the input system if the pointer is click-dragging an object at the time of the event.
Extended or indirect movement information. This covers all types of movement that are unconnected to coordinate positioning - mouse wheel movement and pen tilt are two such examples.
X/Y coordinate movement only. Movement such as the wheel mouse spinning is not covered by this type as it does not influence the coordinate system.
Input is a repeated entry (i.e. user is holding down a button and a repetition timer is being triggered)
Indicates to the receiver of this message that it is not the primary/original recipient
Synonym for L_ALT | R_ALT
Synonym for R_ALT
Caps-Lock is on
Synonym for L_COMMAND | R_COMMAND
Synonym for L_CONTROL | R_CONTROL
Synonym for L_CONTROL | R_CONTROL
This key will be affected by a previously pressed dead-key
Synonym for REPEAT | RELEASED | PRESSED | NOT_PRINTABLE | CAPS_LOCK
Synonym for ALT | CONTROL
Left Alt is held
Left Logo/Special
Control Key is held
Control Key is held
Left Shift is held
The represented key value is not printable
NumLock is on
Identifies numeric keypad keys
Key is being held or tapped
Synonym for SHIFT | COMMAND | ALT | CONTROL
The key is being released
This is a repeated key
Right Alt is held
Right Logo/Special
Control Key is held
Control Key is held
Right Shift is held
Scroll Lock is on
Synonym for L_SHIFT | R_SHIFT

constants.comment: Memory types used by AllocMemory(). The lower 16 bits are stored with allocated blocks, the upper 16 bits are function-relative only.
constants.const: The memory space is reserved by an audio device such as a sound card.

This flag is usable only in routines that are supporting a class method. It forces the memory allocation to be tracked to the object that made the method call. This is particularly important in methods that return memory blocks that do not form a part of the object itself.
Set if the memory will contain executable program code.
The default type, DATA, is used to indicate a standard memory allocation from system RAM.
Hidden blocks are not recorded and are excluded from resource tracking.

Enables custom resource management for the memory block. The start of the block will need to be reserved with a pointer to a ResourceManager structure, which is included as part of the block's declared Size. The Free() callback will be called when the block is removed.
Permanently turn off all accesses to this memory block. This means that multiple threads can have full read/write access to the memory block at once regardless of other acces flags.
Permanently turn off all accesses to this memory block. This means that multiple threads can have full read/write access to the memory block at once regardless of other acces flags.
Do not clear the memory on allocation (saves time).
For AllocMemory() only, indicates that the (private) memory block should not be locked on return.
Gives a hint to the allocator to allocate the block outside of the memory pool.
The memory is explicitly marked as readable.
Synonym for READ | WRITE
Identifies the memory content as a null terminated string. Useful for debugging and run-time type identification in scripts.
The memory space is reserved by a video driver for hosting texture graphics.
Enables temporary locking restrictions. Prevents processes from sleeping while holding a lock on the memory block.
Allocating an untracked memory block will prevent the memory block from being tracked back to the object holding the current context.
The memory space is reserved by a video device such as a graphics card for display purposes, e.g. framebuffer.
The memory is explicitly marked as writeable.
Synonym for EVERYONE_DELETE
Synonym for EVERYONE_EXEC
Synonym for EVERYONE_READ
Synonym for EVERYONE_WRITE
Marks the file for future backup. The flag should be cleared after the backup has succeeded.
Owner can delete. If the file system does not support this, deletion is enabled via the WRITE flag.
Synonym for EVERYONE_READ | EVERYONE_WRITE | EVERYONE_EXEC | EVERYONE_DELETE
Synonym for DELETE | GROUP_DELETE | OTHERS_DELETE
Synonym for EXEC | GROUP_EXEC | OTHERS_EXEC
Synonym for READ | GROUP_READ | OTHERS_READ
Synonym for EVERYONE_READ | EVERYONE_WRITE
Synonym for WRITE | GROUP_WRITE | OTHERS_WRITE
User/Owner can execute.
Synonym for GROUP_READ | GROUP_WRITE | GROUP_EXEC | GROUP_DELETE
Allows executables to run with a set group id.
Group members can delete.
Group members can execute.
Group members can read.
Group members can write.
Recommends that the file is hidden from view by default.
Inherit permissions from parent folder and logical OR them with preset permission flags.
Synonym for HIDDEN | PASSWORD | OFFLINE | NETWORK
File is hosted on another machine.
File content for this networked file has not been cached on the local PC.
Synonym for OTHERS_READ | OTHERS_WRITE | OTHERS_EXEC | OTHERS_DELETE
Others can delete.
Others can execute.
Others can read.
Others can write.
File is password protected.
User/Owner has read access. This will not allow compiled code to be executed.
Synonym for READ | WRITE | EXEC | DELETE
Allows executables to run with a set user id.
Synonym for EXEC
Synonym for READ
Synonym for WRITE
User/Owner can write.

constants.comment: Official version number (date format). Any changes to the handling of document content require that this number be updated.

constants.const: Use an index at the end of the last read/write operation.
Use an index at the end of the data buffer.
The index is between 0 and 1.0 and relative to the data size.
Use an index at position zero.

struct.name: DateTime
struct.field: Year
Month 1 to 12
Day 1 to 31
Hour 0 to 23
Minute 0 to 59
Second 0 to 59
TimeZone -13 to +13

struct.name: InputEvent
struct.field: Next event in the chain
The value associated with the Type
PreciseTime() of the recorded input
Surface that the input message is being conveyed to
Surface that is directly under the mouse pointer at the time of the event
Absolute horizontal position of mouse cursor (relative to the top left of the display)
Absolute vertical position of mouse cursor (relative to the top left of the display)
Horizontal position relative to the surface that the pointer is over - unless a mouse button is held or pointer is anchored - then the coordinates are relative to the click-held surface
Vertical position relative to the surface that the pointer is over - unless a mouse button is held or pointer is anchored - then the coordinates are relative to the click-held surface
The hardware device that this event originated from
JET constant that describes the event
Broad descriptors for the given Type (see JTYPE flags). Automatically defined when delivered to the pointer object
Mask to use for checking against subscribers

struct.name: dcAudio
struct.field: Byte size of this structure
Format of the audio data

struct.name: dcDeviceInput
struct.field: The value(s) associated with the Type
PreciseTime of the recorded input
The hardware device that this event originated from (note: This ID can be to a private/inaccessible object, the point is that the ID is unique)
Broad descriptors for the given Type. Automatically defined when delivered to the pointer object
JET constant

struct.name: dcKeyEntry
struct.field: Shift/Control/CapsLock...
ASCII value of the key A/B/C/D...
PreciseTime at which the keypress was recorded
Unicode value for pre-calculated key translations

struct.name: dcRequest
struct.field: Identifier for retrieval from the source
Data preferences for the returned item(s)
